findings: Species identity is supported by the yellow spectacles, bright yellow throat and upper breast, olive upperparts, gray rump, two white wingbars, white belly, and compact vireo form; these agree with the reference photos and verified guides.
All visible taxonomy, measurement, habitat, behavior, and field-mark claims are correct. The plate contains no place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail.

The bird is depicted with one head, one beak, two wings (one largely occluded in profile), two legs, and one tail. The bill is slightly longer and finer-looking than the stout, subtly hooked bill in the photos, but remains acceptable and is not a material identification error.
